description: Hands-on Kubernetes for practitioners who already know the basics. Covers configuration management, persistent storage, health checks, zero-downtime deployments, resource management, autoscaling, and Ingress-based traffic routing.
summary: Hands-on Kubernetes for practitioners who already know the basics. Covers configuration management, persistent storage, health checks, zero-downtime deployments, resource management, autoscaling, and Ingress-based traffic routing.
target_audience: Learners who are already familiar with Kubernetes fundamentals — Pods, Deployments, Services, and YAML manifests — and want to take the next step toward production-grade operations and deployment reliability.

lectures.title: Separating Configuration with ConfigMap and Secret - Decoupling Code from Config
objectives: Understand why environment variables and configuration files must be separated from the container image (12-factor app)
Distinguish the difference between ConfigMap and Secret and know when to use each
Inject ConfigMap and Secret into Pods via environment variables and volume mounts

lectures.title: Persistent Volumes (PV/PVC) and Storage - Keeping Your Data Alive
objectives: Understand the problem of data loss on Pod restart and why persistent storage is necessary
Explain the relationship between PersistentVolume, PersistentVolumeClaim, and StorageClass
Create a PVC, mount it in a Pod, and verify data persistence

lectures.title: Health Checks (Probes) and Zero-Downtime Deployment - Rolling Updates and Rollback Strategies
objectives: Distinguish the roles and differences of Liveness, Readiness, and Startup Probes
Understand how maxSurge and maxUnavailable control rolling update behavior
Perform a rollback with kubectl rollout and manage deployment history when a release fails

lectures.title: Resource Management and Autoscaling - Requests/Limits and HPA
objectives: Understand how CPU/memory requests and limits affect scheduling, QoS class, and OOMKill behavior
Explain how the Horizontal Pod Autoscaler adjusts Pod count based on load
Configure an HPA backed by metrics-server and verify its behavior under a load test

lectures.title: Routing External Traffic with Ingress - Layer 7 Routing and Domain-Based Access
objectives: Understand the limitations of NodePort and LoadBalancer and why Ingress is needed
Explain the relationship between an Ingress Controller and Ingress resources, including host- and path-based routing rules
Write an Ingress manifest to expose multiple services through a single entry point
